Question: What are the primary business pain points with the current manual EO Framework documentation process?
Guidance: Describe top 3-5 inefficiencies — e.g., rework loops, LLM iteration cycles, time lost per engagement
Notes: Baseline for justifying automation investment and scoping the MVP

Question: How many presales engagements does Amatra run per month today, and what is the projected growth rate over the next 12 months?
Guidance: Provide current monthly volume and expected volume at 6-month and 12-month marks
Notes: Drives capacity planning for agent concurrency, DynamoDB quota design, and Bedrock token budgeting

Question: What measurable success criteria will determine whether this platform is considered a success at go-live?
Guidance: Examples: end-to-end generation in under 60 minutes, 90% reduction in senior-consultant hours, zero manual retries per artifact
Notes: Critical for acceptance testing and executive-sponsor sign-off

Question: What is the acceptable per-artifact validation failure rate in production?
Guidance: Current state has 3-4 LLM iteration retries; what is the target pass-on-first-attempt rate?
Notes: Informs quality-gate thresholds and the LLM retry cap (currently 3)

Question: Is the Q2 2026 / end-of-April PoC deadline fixed, and what are the consequences of a slip?
Guidance: Confirm executive-demonstration date; identify contractual or budget-cycle dependencies
Notes: Hard deadline shapes phase sequencing and MVP scope

Question: Are the three delivery phases (Weeks 1-4, 5-9, 10-12) fixed, or can they be re-baselined if scope is reduced?
Guidance: Identify which phase milestones have external dependencies (e.g., Cognito approval, AWS account provisioning)
Notes: Impacts resource loading and sprint planning

Question: What is the approved budget envelope for the 12-week build engagement versus the first-year run cost?
Guidance: Confirm the $250k professional-services and $100k-$200k run-cost split; identify who controls each budget line
Notes: Procurement trigger and change-order thresholds

Question: At what monthly AWS spend level does procurement need to be re-engaged?
Guidance: Identify the existing AWS spend envelope and the threshold that triggers a new procurement cycle
Notes: Determines whether platform spend can be absorbed into existing agreement or requires a new commitment

Question: Who has authority to approve production deployment of the Cognito-based user pool?
Guidance: Identify the CTO and any security/compliance reviewers who must sign off
Notes: Approval dependency that could block Phase 1 completion

Question: Who are the end users of the CLI and HTTP API — internal consultants only, or also external clients?
Guidance: Define the user population: Amatra consultants, PREDICTif engineers, or customer-facing personas
Notes: Drives Cognito pool design, per-user quota policy, and UX requirements

Question: What is the current fully-loaded cost (consultant time + overhead) to produce one presales package manually?
Guidance: Use the 6-10 senior-consultant-hours estimate as a baseline; confirm the blended hourly rate
Notes: Quantifies ROI baseline for the business case and executive presentation

Question: Is parallel pipeline throughput (multiple solutions generated simultaneously) a hard requirement or a stretch goal?
Guidance: Confirm whether concurrent multi-solution generation is needed for the PoC demo or Phase 2
Notes: Architectural decision driver for Lambda concurrency limits and AgentCore scaling

Question: Describe the existing AWS account structure — single account or multi-account org, and which account will host the new platform?
Guidance: Confirm whether a net-new us-west-2 account is being created or whether an existing account is being used with a new region footprint
Notes: Foundation for IAM, VPC, and blast-radius decisions

Question: What AWS services are currently active in the us-east-1 footprint, and are any shared resources (IAM roles, S3 buckets, VPCs) expected to be reused?
Guidance: List managed-services workloads and identify any cross-region or cross-account dependencies
Notes: Isolation boundary design and blast-radius analysis

Question: Has the team used Amazon Bedrock or AgentCore Runtime in any previous engagement?
Guidance: Identify existing Bedrock model access approvals, service quotas, and any prior AgentCore Runtime experience
Notes: Bedrock model access (Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5) must be enabled before Phase 2; quota increases may have lead time

Question: What is the target AWS region for each service — is us-west-2 confirmed for all services including Bedrock, Cognito, and API Gateway?
Guidance: Confirm regional availability of Bedrock model IDs (claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5) in us-west-2
Notes: Some Bedrock model IDs may only be available in specific regions; verify before architecture is finalised

Question: How many concurrent solution-generation workflows must the platform support at steady state (200/month) and at peak?
Guidance: Estimate peak concurrency — e.g., 10 simultaneous solutions — to size Lambda reserved concurrency and AgentCore Runtime instances
Notes: Drives Lambda concurrency limits, DynamoDB throughput mode, and Bedrock token-per-minute quota

Question: Confirm the five-agent topology: Input Validator (Agent 0), Pre-Sales Generator, Delivery Generator, Code Generator, EO Validator — is this fixed or subject to change?
Guidance: Identify any additional agents planned (e.g., cost estimator, diagram generator) that should be architected in from the start
Notes: Agent count affects AgentCore Runtime registration, IAM role design, and per-solution token budget

Question: What is the expected average token count per artifact and per full solution package (12 artifacts)?
Guidance: Provide estimates for input + output tokens per artifact type (e.g., solution-briefing vs. Terraform bundle)
Notes: Primary driver of Bedrock cost model and per-solution $5 spend target feasibility

Question: Describe the 14 CLI subcommands and 11 HTTP API routes — which are in-scope for the PoC and which are deferred?
Guidance: List the four core capabilities required for the April demo: CLI auth, presales generation, delivery generation, automation bundle
Notes: Scope boundary for Phase 3 testing and acceptance

Question: What authentication flow does the CLI use — device-code flow, username/password, or machine-to-machine client credentials?
Guidance: Confirm the Cognito auth flow (USER_PASSWORD_AUTH, DEVICE_FLOW, or MACHINE_TO_MACHINE) for the pip-installable CLI
Notes: Cognito app-client configuration and token-refresh design (30-day refresh tokens confirmed)

Question: What is the expected S3 storage volume at steady state (200 solutions/month) — raw artifacts, converted DOCX/PPTX/XLSX, and GitHub-committed bundles?
Guidance: Estimate average solution package size (raw + converted) multiplied by monthly volume and retention period
Notes: S3 storage cost and lifecycle-policy design

Question: What is the DynamoDB access pattern for quota enforcement — single-table design with per-user and global counters, or separate tables?
Guidance: Confirm atomic counter strategy: DynamoDB transactions or conditional writes for the 10/user/month and 1000/month global quota
Notes: DynamoDB capacity mode (on-demand vs. provisioned) and consistency requirements

Question: Describe the eof-tools converter library interface — is it invoked as a Python subprocess, imported as a module, or called via an internal API?
Guidance: Confirm how the 30-module library covering 12 artifact types is packaged into the agent container image
Notes: Container image size, cold-start latency, and dependency management

Question: Are all 12 artifact types supported by the current eof-tools library, or are there gaps that require new converter modules?
Guidance: Identify any artifact types (e.g., new Terraform bundle format) not yet covered by eof-tools
Notes: Scope risk — new converter modules may add to Phase 2 effort

Question: What GitHub repository structure is expected — one repo per solution, one repo per client, or a single monorepo with solution-scoped directories?
Guidance: Confirm the fixed public repository mentioned in the brief and the branching strategy for committed artifacts
Notes: GitHub personal-access-token scope, repo permissions, and CI/CD trigger design

Question: How is the GitHub personal access token managed — AWS Secrets Manager, Parameter Store, or environment variable in the container?
Guidance: Identify rotation policy and who owns the token
Notes: Security and operational risk; token expiry could silently break artifact delivery

Question: What is the maximum acceptable end-to-end latency for a full 12-artifact solution generation run?
Guidance: Confirm the under-60-minute target; identify whether this is a p50, p95, or p99 target
Notes: Lambda timeout configuration (max 15 min per function) and Step Functions / async orchestration design

Question: What are the Terraform validation gate requirements — is `terraform validate` the only gate, or are `terraform plan` dry-runs also required?
Guidance: Identify whether a live AWS account is needed for plan execution or whether validate-only is sufficient for the PoC
Notes: Infrastructure and IAM requirements for Terraform execution environment

Question: What CloudWatch metrics baseline is required for Phase 3 sign-off?
Guidance: Define the specific metrics: per-agent token usage, artifact generation latency, validation pass/fail rates, quota utilisation
Notes: Monitoring design and CloudWatch dashboard scope

Question: Is distributed tracing (AWS X-Ray) required, or is CloudWatch Logs correlation sufficient for the PoC?
Guidance: Confirm observability tooling preferences and any existing monitoring stack
Notes: Tracing instrumentation adds latency and cost; confirm if required for PoC

Question: What is the long-term throughput target beyond the 200-solutions/month steady state — is there a 12-month or 24-month growth projection?
Guidance: Provide peak throughput scenarios (e.g., 500 or 1000 solutions/month) to validate the linear-scaling architecture claim
Notes: Architecture validation for Lambda concurrency, DynamoDB, and Bedrock quota ceilings

Question: What compliance frameworks apply to this platform and its generated artifacts?
Guidance: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, or others — particularly relevant if customer data flows through the platform
Notes: Determines audit-logging depth, encryption requirements, and penetration-testing scope

Question: What is the sensitivity classification of the presales artifacts and briefing data processed by the platform?
Guidance: Classify as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted — presales documents may contain client pricing and architecture IP
Notes: Drives encryption-at-rest configuration, S3 bucket policies, and data-retention rules

Question: Beyond Cognito MFA, are there additional authentication requirements such as SSO federation with an enterprise IdP (Okta, Azure AD)?
Guidance: Confirm whether SAML 2.0 or OIDC federation is required for Amatra consultants or PREDICTif corporate accounts
Notes: Cognito identity pool vs. user pool design; federation adds configuration complexity

Question: What is the role-based access control model — admin, standard user, and read-only reviewer, or a different model?
Guidance: Define the permission boundaries: who can create solutions, who can view all solutions, who can manage quotas
Notes: IAM and Cognito group configuration; admin usage endpoint access control

Question: Must all data (S3 artifacts, DynamoDB records, CloudWatch logs) remain in us-west-2, or are cross-region replicas permitted?
Guidance: Confirm data-residency requirements for any client-specific content processed by the platform
Notes: S3 replication, CloudWatch log export, and DynamoDB global table design

Question: What encryption requirements apply to data at rest and in transit?
Guidance: Confirm: S3 SSE-KMS vs. SSE-S3, DynamoDB encryption at rest, TLS 1.2+ for API Gateway, KMS key ownership (AWS-managed vs. customer-managed)
Notes: Customer-managed KMS keys add operational overhead; confirm if required for compliance

Question: Is the Lambda and AgentCore Runtime environment required to run inside a VPC, or is public-endpoint execution acceptable?
Guidance: Identify any network egress restrictions or VPC endpoint requirements for Bedrock, S3, and DynamoDB
Notes: VPC configuration significantly increases cold-start latency and adds NAT Gateway cost

Question: What audit-logging requirements exist — CloudTrail for all API calls, S3 access logs, and DynamoDB stream logging?
Guidance: Confirm log retention periods and whether logs must be exported to a SIEM
Notes: Audit log volume and cost; SIEM integration adds to Phase 1 scope

Question: How are sensitive credentials managed — GitHub PAT, Bedrock API keys, Cognito client secrets?
Guidance: Confirm use of AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation vs. Parameter Store SecureString
Notes: Rotation policy and access audit trail for all platform secrets

Question: What is the container image vulnerability scanning policy for the AgentCore Runtime Docker image containing eof-tools?
Guidance: Identify whether Amazon ECR image scanning (basic or enhanced via Inspector) is required at build and runtime
Notes: Container security posture; critical vulnerabilities in eof-tools dependencies could block deployment

Question: What is the Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the platform if a regional AWS service disruption occurs?
Guidance: Define maximum acceptable downtime: under 1 hour, 4 hours, or next business day
Notes: Determines whether multi-AZ or multi-region failover is required; impacts architecture cost

Question: What is the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) for DynamoDB quota counters and S3 solution artifacts?
Guidance: Define maximum acceptable data loss: zero (point-in-time recovery) or last daily backup
Notes: DynamoDB PITR and S3 versioning configuration; PITR adds ~20% storage cost

Question: What CloudWatch alarms are required at launch — Lambda error rates, Bedrock throttling, API Gateway 5xx rates, DynamoDB capacity?
Guidance: Define alerting thresholds and notification channels (SNS → PagerDuty, email, Slack)
Notes: Operational readiness gate for Phase 3 sign-off

Question: Is a real-time operational dashboard required for the PoC demo, or is CloudWatch Logs Insights sufficient?
Guidance: Identify whether a CloudWatch Dashboard, Grafana, or third-party APM tool is expected
Notes: Dashboard scope affects Phase 3 timeline; confirm for demo readiness

Question: What support coverage is required for the platform post-launch — business hours 8x5, extended 24x5, or 24x7?
Guidance: Identify the on-call rotation and escalation path for P1 incidents (e.g., platform down during peak sales cycle)
Notes: Managed-service scope definition and staffing cost estimate

Question: What are acceptable maintenance windows for Lambda deployments and DynamoDB schema changes?
Guidance: Identify days/times when the platform can be taken offline for updates; serverless architecture minimises downtime but blue/green deployments must be planned
Notes: CI/CD pipeline design and change-management process

Question: What is the artifact retention policy — how long must completed solution packages be stored in S3?
Guidance: Define retention period (e.g., 90 days, 1 year, indefinite) and whether expired artifacts should be archived to S3 Glacier
Notes: S3 lifecycle policy design and storage cost projection

Question: How will per-user (10/month) and global (1000/month) quota limits be monitored and adjusted over time?
Guidance: Define the operational process for quota increases: self-service admin API, manual DynamoDB update, or automated scaling
Notes: Quota management runbook and admin tooling scope

Question: What AWS Cost Explorer tags or cost-allocation tags are required to track platform spend by team, project, or client?
Guidance: Identify tagging strategy for Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, Bedrock, and Cognito resources
Notes: Cost visibility and chargeback model; required if spend is allocated across multiple business units

Question: Is this a greenfield deployment (no existing platform to migrate from) or does it need to coexist with the current manual Claude Code CLI process during a transition period?
Guidance: Confirm whether a parallel-run period is required and its minimum duration
Notes: If parallel run is needed, both old and new processes must be supported simultaneously, increasing Phase 2 scope

Question: Confirm the Phase 1 deliverables (Weeks 1-4): Cognito, API Gateway, DynamoDB, S3, and baseline IAM — are there any additions or removals?
Guidance: Identify any pre-requisites not in the brief: AWS account creation, VPC setup, DNS, or certificate management
Notes: Phase 1 is the foundation; scope changes here cascade to Phase 2 and 3 timelines

Question: Confirm Phase 2 deliverables (Weeks 5-9): Strands Agents, AgentCore Runtime registration, Docker image pipeline, eof-tools integration — are all five agents in scope?
Guidance: Identify which agents (Input Validator, Pre-Sales Generator, Delivery Generator, Code Generator, EO Validator) are required for the PoC demo vs. phased delivery
Notes: Agent count and complexity are the primary Phase 2 risk driver

Question: What internal Amatra/PREDICTif resources are available for this engagement — dedicated engineers, part-time SMEs, and executive sponsor time?
Guidance: Identify team roles: AWS architect, backend developer, DevOps/IaC engineer, QA lead, and project manager; confirm time commitment percentages
Notes: Resource availability is the primary schedule risk; confirm full-time vs. fractional allocation

Question: Does PREDICTif have AWS Advanced or Premier tier partnership with access to AWS ProServe or Partner Development resources?
Guidance: Confirm APN tier, available AWS credits (MAP, Partner Opportunity), and access to AWS Bedrock specialists
Notes: AWS partner resources could accelerate Bedrock and AgentCore Runtime integration in Phase 2

Question: Is Terraform the mandated IaC tool for all platform infrastructure, or is AWS CDK or CloudFormation acceptable for specific components?
Guidance: Confirm IaC tooling standards; identify whether Terraform Cloud/Enterprise or open-source Terraform with S3 backend is used
Notes: Terraform state management design and `terraform validate` gate implementation

Question: What CI/CD platform is required — GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline, GitLab CI, or Jenkins?
Guidance: Confirm the pipeline tooling and branching strategy for infrastructure and application code
Notes: CI/CD pipeline design is a Phase 2 dependency for Docker image builds and Terraform deployments

Question: What is the User Acceptance Testing (UAT) plan for Phase 3 — which artifact types, how many test solutions, and who signs off?
Guidance: Define the 12-artifact green run criteria: all 5 presales artifacts, 6 delivery artifacts, and 1 automation bundle must pass format-check and LLM quality-check
Notes: Phase 3 acceptance gate; confirm sign-off authority (Marcus Patel, Daniel Park, or CTO)

Question: What training is required for Amatra consultants using the CLI and for administrators managing quotas and monitoring?
Guidance: Identify training format: live workshop, recorded video, runbook documentation, or hands-on lab
Notes: Training scope affects Phase 3 timeline and knowledge-transfer deliverables

Question: What are the go-live readiness criteria for the April PoC demonstration — specific metrics, artifact types, or stakeholder sign-offs?
Guidance: Define the minimum viable demo: CLI auth flow, one full presales generation, one delivery generation, and one Terraform bundle with validate gate passing
Notes: Executive-sponsor demo script and acceptance criteria

Question: What is the risk if Bedrock model access for Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Claude Haiku 4.5 is not approved in us-west-2 before Phase 2 begins?
Guidance: Identify the model access request process, approval lead time, and fallback model options if specific model IDs are unavailable
Notes: Model access approval is a hard dependency for Phase 2; a 2-4 week lead time could compress the Phase 2 window

Question: What is the contingency plan if AgentCore Runtime is not generally available or has breaking API changes before the PoC deadline?
Guidance: Identify fallback orchestration options: ECS-hosted Strands agents, Step Functions, or Lambda-direct invocation
Notes: AgentCore Runtime is a relatively new service; GA stability and regional availability should be confirmed early

Question: Are there external dependencies on AWS Professional Services, Anthropic support, or third-party Strands Agents documentation that could introduce delays?
Guidance: List all vendor or partner dependencies with expected response SLAs
Notes: Vendor dependency lead times should be mapped to the Phase 2 critical path

Question: Are there any organisational constraints on open-source library usage within the platform (e.g., Strands Agents framework licensing)?
Guidance: Review open-source licence compliance requirements for Strands Agents, eof-tools, and any third-party Python dependencies in the container image
Notes: Licence risk could block deployment to production if legal review is required

Question: Confirm that the eof-tools converter library is production-ready for all 12 artifact types and does not require significant refactoring for containerised execution.
Guidance: Identify any known issues with eof-tools running in a headless Lambda/container environment (e.g., font rendering, LibreOffice dependencies for DOCX conversion)
Notes: eof-tools readiness is a critical Phase 2 assumption; undocumented dependencies (LibreOffice, fonts) in a container can cause significant rework
